introSection: Diamond Back is a steel launched shuttle roller coaster manufactured by Arrow Development that is currently located at Frontier City in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, USA. It originally operated at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA from 1978 to 1987 as the lower coaster of Lightnin' Loops.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Lightnin' Loops opened at Six Flags Great Adventure in 1978. The attraction consisted of two separate roller coasters with interlocking loops, with a lower loop (this article) and an upper loop.

The lower loop closed on June 17, 1987 after a major accident occurred on the upper loop, which resulted in decreased ride popularity and low park attendance not sufficient enough for operation. It remained standing but not operating until both rides were removed after the 1992 season to make room for Batman The Ride. The rides were sold to Tierco Group, Inc., which separated the attractions. The lower loop was relocated to Frontier City in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, USA, where it reopened in 1994 as Diamond Back. The upper loop was relocated to Adventure World, where it reopened in 1993 as Python.

Diamond Back was standing but not operating from 2024 through mid-2025. Despite park management stating in March 2025 that the ride was closed for the season, it reopened in June.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Blue/yellow track and yellow supports, used to be black track with white supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single train with 6 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 4 riders per car or 24 riders per train.

fullText:
Diamond Back is a steel launched shuttle roller coaster manufactured by Arrow Development that is currently located at Frontier City in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, USA. It originally operated at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA from 1978 to 1987 as the lower coaster of Lightnin' Loops.

Lightnin' Loops opened at Six Flags Great Adventure in 1978. The attraction consisted of two separate roller coasters with interlocking loops, with a lower loop (this article) and an upper loop.

The lower loop closed on June 17, 1987 after a major accident occurred on the upper loop, which resulted in decreased ride popularity and low park attendance not sufficient enough for operation. It remained standing but not operating until both rides were removed after the 1992 season to make room for Batman The Ride. The rides were sold to Tierco Group, Inc., which separated the attractions. The lower loop was relocated to Frontier City in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, USA, where it reopened in 1994 as Diamond Back. The upper loop was relocated to Adventure World, where it reopened in 1993 as Python.

Diamond Back was standing but not operating from 2024 through mid-2025. Despite park management stating in March 2025 that the ride was closed for the season, it reopened in June.



Blue/yellow track and yellow supports, used to be black track with white supports.

Single train with 6 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 4 riders per car or 24 riders per train.
